stories.title: Green says Mets fully compliant on AI after report of usage
stories.simple_summary: The Mets baseball team checked their use of AI tools and confirmed they're following all the rules.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to sports fans and the Mets organization — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese AI company released a new version of their AI helper, and some people are worried about how it compares to American AI tools.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies tracking competition — it doesn't change what AI tools you can use today.

stories.title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
stories.simple_summary: A tech magazine published its usual guide recommending helpful apps and gadgets to readers.
stories.why_it_matters: If you read tech reviews, this might have some useful tool recommendations, but there's no AI news here specifically.

stories.title: Moonshot’s Kimi Upends Conventional Wisdom on US Lead Over China
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese AI company's new tool is performing better than expected, which challenges the idea that American companies are always ahead.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and business strategists — the AI tools you use should continue working the same.

stories.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
stories.simple_summary: A wealthy investor predicts that the huge profits being made from AI will eventually need to be shared more widely with society.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and policymakers — it's about money redistribution, not anything affecting how you'd use AI.

stories.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
stories.simple_summary: A luxury phone company is selling a personal AI assistant feature for nearly $7,000, and reviewers tested whether it's actually worth that price.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you're thinking about buying an extremely expensive luxury phone, this doesn't affect you — it's a niche product.

stories.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
stories.simple_summary: A data company called Databricks is now worth $188 billion because it switched to focusing on AI tools, especially ones that help write computer code.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it shows the AI industry thinks certain tools are very valuable, but doesn't change what's available to you.

stories.title: What to know about the AI chip stock selloff - ABC News
stories.source: Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
stories.simple_summary: Stocks of companies that make AI computer chips dropped in price recently.
stories.why_it_matters: If you own these stocks, pay attention to this; otherwise it's mainly investor news about market changes.

stories.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
stories.simple_summary: There's a feature that stops Zoom video calls from being recorded and transcribed automatically, raising questions about whether recording everything is useful.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Zoom for work or meetings, this is worth knowing about — you can now opt out of automatic recording if you want privacy.

stories.title: China’s Xi Jinping Wants AI to Be Open to the World—and Out of America’s Control
stories.simple_summary: China's leader says he wants AI technology to be available to countries around the world and not controlled only by America.
stories.why_it_matters: This is political news about countries competing for power — it doesn't directly change what AI tools you can use today.

stories.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
stories.simple_summary: A robot company is opening a training facility in California to teach their robot helpers to do warehouse and delivery jobs.
stories.why_it_matters: This signals that AI robots are being tested for real warehouse work, which could eventually affect job availability in shipping and delivery, but it's still very early.

stories.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
stories.simple_summary: India's smartphone market is slowing down because many new phones now need extra memory for AI features, which is making them more expensive.
stories.why_it_matters: If you buy smartphones, this suggests prices may stay higher as companies add AI features — phones are becoming costlier to make.
